# Migra fixture: identitycols

Lifted from `pgkit/packages/migra/test/fixtures/identitycols/`.

## identitycols

<details>
<summary>input</summary>

```sql (a.sql)
create table t(
    a int,
    b int default 1,
    c int generated always as (1) stored,
    d int generated always as identity,
    e int generated by default as identity
);


create table "public"."gen" (
    "adding" integer,
    "removing" integer generated always as (1) stored
);


create table identchanges (
    c int default 77,
    d int generated always as identity,
    e int generated by default as identity,
    f int generated always as identity
);
```

```sql (b.sql)
create table t2(
    a int,
    b int default 1,
    c int generated always as (1) stored,
    d int generated always as identity,
    e int generated by default as identity
);

create table "public"."gen" (
    "adding" integer generated always as (1) stored,
    "removing" integer
);


create table identchanges (
    c int generated by default as identity,
    e int generated always as identity,
    d int generated by default as identity,
    f int
);
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>output</summary>

```sql (expected.sql)
drop table "public"."t";

create table "public"."t2" (
    "a" integer,
    "b" integer default 1,
    "c" integer generated always as (1) stored,
    "d" integer generated always as identity not null,
    "e" integer generated by default as identity not null
);


alter table "public"."gen" drop column "adding";

alter table "public"."gen" add column "adding" integer generated always as (1) stored;

alter table "public"."gen" alter column "removing" drop expression;

alter table "public"."identchanges" alter column "c" drop default;

alter table "public"."identchanges" alter column "c" set not null;

alter table "public"."identchanges" alter column "c" add generated by default as identity;

alter table "public"."identchanges" alter column "d" set generated by default;

alter table "public"."identchanges" alter column "e" set generated always;

alter table "public"."identchanges" alter column "f" drop identity;

alter table "public"."identchanges" alter column "f" drop not null;
```

</details>
